                   MANDATE, REQUEST FOR STAY / PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND REQUEST FOR

              Ryan M. Keever (State Bar No. 301204)           ELECTRONICALLY        
              Frances E. Heredia (State Bar No. 352103)         F I L E D           
              7355 Morro Road, Suite 101                      Superior Court of California,
              Atascadero, California 93422                     County of San Francisco
              805.547.9300 (telephone)                          10/29/2024          
              805.547.9302 (facsimile)                        Clerk of the Court    
              info@simasgovlaw.com                             BY: JUDITH NUNEZ     
                                                                     Deputy Clerk   
              Attorneys for Petitioner Jialiang Dong                                
                           SUPERIOR COURT OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA                
                                   COUNTY  OF SAN FRANCISCO                         
              JIALIANG DONG, an individual;   Case No.: CPF-24-518677               
                         Petitioners,         FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR            
                                              WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE,       
                    vs.                       WRIT OF ORDINARY MANDATE,             
                                              REQUEST  FOR STAY / PRELIMINARY       
              UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO     INJUNCTION AND REQUEST                
              SCHOOL  OF LAW,                 FOR DECLARATORY  RELIEF               
                                              (Code Civ. Proc., §§ 1094.5, 1085, 1176, and Respondent.
                                              1060)                                 
                                              Judge:                                
                                              Dept.:                                
                  Petitioner Jialiang Dong, (“Mr. Dong” or “Petitioner”) hereby respectfully petitions this
              Court to issue a writ of ordinary mandate pursuant to Section 1085, administrative mandate under
              Section 1094.5, and declaratory relief under Section 1060 of the California Code of Civil Procedure
              directing the University of San Francisco – School of Law (“USF”) to review the arbitrary and
              capricious decision of USF suspending Mr. Dong, to compel USF to reinstate Mr. Dong from
              suspension in light of USF’s abuses of discretion and failure to follow their own rules and
              processes, and to stay the impact of his suspension pending the final adjudication of this petition by
              this stay request, Petitioner respectfully requests the Court to stay the imposition of the suspension
              to allow him to enroll in courses at USF until the merits of the writ are heard before this Court.

                                               2                                    
                                                                                    
               FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE, WRIT OF ORDINARY
                   MANDATE, REQUEST FOR STAY / PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND REQUEST FOR
                                       DECLARATORY RELIEF                           
   .DTL                                                                             
   ,SETAICOSSA                                                                      
   &                                                                                
   SAMIS                                                                            
    WAL                                                                             
    TA                                                                              
    SYENROTTA                                                                       
                                    JURISDICTION AND VENUE                          
                  1.   This Court has jurisdiction to consider this petition pursuant to California Code of
              Civil Procedure § 1085 and § 1094.5.                                  
                  2.   USF which issued its suspension of Petitioner is located at 2130 Fulton Street, San
              Francisco, California 94117, in San Francisco County and the Superior Court of the State of
              California, County of San Francisco is thus the location where the dispute arose. San Francisco
              County is the appropriate venue. (Code Civ. Proc., § 393.)            
                                           PARTIES                                  
                  3.   Petitioner Mr. Dong is a resident of California and a third-year law student at USF.
                  4.   Respondent USF is now, and at all times mentioned in this petition has been, a
              private, nonprofit university established in 1855, and the School of Law was founded in 1912. It is
              accredited by the American Bar Association and is a member of the Association of American Law
              Schools.1                                                             
                                    GENERAL  ALLEGATIONS                            
                  5.   For nearly three years, Mr. Dong has been a law student at USF. He is paying tuition
              of $27,500 per semester, earning his legal education, and working towards becoming a licensed
              attorney in the State of California. During his time at USF, Mr. Dong has been in good standing,
              excelled in his courses, and received the Dean Scholarship for academic excellence.
                  6.   On December 7, 2023, Mr. Dong submitted a paper as a final assignment in a
              required USF course, Art Law, to USF Professor Karren Shorofsky (“Professor Shorofsky”).
                  7.   Professor Shorofsky ran “plagiarism detection software”, Turnitin, on Mr. Dong’s
              paper. The Turnitin report reflected a “similarity index” score of 41%. However, the Turnitin report
              included borrowed text within quotation marks, and while the referenced materials were cited in the
              bibliography, was a potential violation of the USF Honor Code. (Committee Report issued June 4,
              2024, at p.1)                                                         
              1 https://www.usfca.edu/law/our-difference/quick-facts

               FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE, WRIT OF ORDINARY
                   MANDATE, REQUEST FOR STAY / PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND REQUEST FOR
                                       DECLARATORY RELIEF                           
   .DTL                                                                             
   ,SETAICOSSA                                                                      
   &                                                                                
   SAMIS                                                                            
    WAL                                                                             
    TA                                                                              
    SYENROTTA                                                                       
                  8.   Professor Shorofsky reported Mr. Dong to Associate Dean Amy Flynn (“Dean
              Flynn”) via email for a potential violation of the USF law school honor code and accused Mr. Dong
              of plagiarism. Professor Shorofsky enclosed the similarity report as the basis for the potential
              violation. (Committee Report issued June 4, 2024, at p.2)             
                  9.   Mr. Dong defended his allegations to Professor Shorofsky, noting that he did cite the
              author, apologizing for the problems leading to the misattribution, acknowledging his lack of
              understanding of the rules related to plagiarism in this context, from his prior legal writing courses.
              (Id.)                                                                 
                  10.  On January 9, 2024, Mr. Dong met with Dean Flynn to discuss the allegation and try
              and resolve the matter informally, but no resolution was reached. (Id.)
                  11.  On February 5, 2024, Dean Flynn emailed Mr. Dong an offer of settlement that
              included terms of placing an honor violation letter in Mr. Dong’s USF personnel file, notice that the
              school will turn over the letter to the California State Bar upon for the moral character application,
              however, the letter will be removed from Mr. Dong’s file if he completes his Juris Doctorate
              without further incident and no other honor code violations. Mr. Dong was also instructed to re-
              write the final paper, with a loss of 2/3 credit.                     
                  12.  Mr. Dong met with Dean Flynn on February 16, 2024, to discuss the allegation and
              informal offer of resolution. Mr. Dong was informed that the offer was conditioned upon him
              signing a formal release, discharging USF for any claims by Mr. Dong related to the honor code
              violation. Additionally, Mr. Dong would have to admit to plagiarism, which he strongly denies. Mr.
              Dong rejected the informal resolution.                                
                  13.  In February 2024, Dean Flynn appointed investigators USF Professor Lara Bazelon
              and USF student Jackie Nemanish (collectively “investigators”) to investigate Mr. Dong’s matter.
              (Id.)                                                                 
                  14.  The investigators were advised by Dean Flynn that after they conclude their
              investigation, to either refer the matter to the Honor Committee or come to a “negotiated

               FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE, WRIT OF ORDINARY
                   MANDATE, REQUEST FOR STAY / PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND REQUEST FOR
                                       DECLARATORY RELIEF                           
   .DTL                                                                             
   ,SETAICOSSA                                                                      
   &                                                                                
   SAMIS                                                                            
    WAL                                                                             
    TA                                                                              
    SYENROTTA                                                                       
              disposition” with Mr. Dong. Recommending closing the investigation with no findings of an honor
              code violation was not an option Dean Flynn provided the investigators. (Id.)
                  15.  Mr. Dong was notified of the investigation on February 27, 2024, citing his alleged
              violation of USF Honor Code Section 4.1(b)(3). (Id.)                  
                  16.  Mr. Dong provided a written response on February 28, 2024, and met with the
              investigators via Zoom on March 12, 2024.                             
                  17.  On March 25, 2024, attorney Negin Yamini noticed her representation of Mr. Dong
              to the investigators and the Honor Committee. (Id.)                   
                  18.  On May 14, 2024, Mr. Dong was represented by counsel Ms. Yamini at an
              administrative hearing before the Honor Committee where the testimony of Professor Shorofsky,
              Dean Flynn, and Mr. Dong was heard, and the USF investigators provided argument. (Id.)
                  19.  Evidence presented at hearing consisted of:                  
                       Mr. Dong’s paper after it was run through Turnitin by USF; Mr. Dong’s paper after it
                       was run through Turnitin by a USF Librarian (3-25-24); Mary LaFrance’s article;
                       Mr. Dong’s paper annotated by Professor Shorofsky; Professor Shorofsky’s syllabus;
                       Mr. Dong’s written response to the allegations; Mr. Dong’s March 13, 2024 email;
                       email exchanges contained in Exhibits A-D of the complaint; Dean Flynn’s Honor
                       Code Presentation; Honor Code Agreement signed by Mr. Dong (August 16, 2022);
                       Mr. Dong’s LRWA paper with comments from Professor Edith Ho. 
                       (Id – at footnote No. 2.)                                    
              After hearing, Mr. Dong’s counsel also provided a submission in his support.
                  20.  Mr. Dong proffered his mid-term paper from the USF required course, Legal
              Research, Writing, and Analysis (“LRWA”), taught by USF Professor Edith Ho (“Professor Ho”).
              In the comments from Professor Ho noted, “You are quoting from the editor notes of Campbell.
              This is not good. You need to quote only from the court opinion because the editor notes are not
              primary authorities. Never quote when it is not actual law.” (Id at p. 4, emphasis added)
                  21.  Mr. Dong explained that English was not his first language, he was raised in another
              country, and that he mistakenly took his legal writing teacher, Professor Ho’s verbatim comment to

                                               5                                    
                                                                                    
               FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE, WRIT OF ORDINARY
                   MANDATE, REQUEST FOR STAY / PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND REQUEST FOR
                                       DECLARATORY RELIEF                           
   .DTL                                                                             
   ,SETAICOSSA                                                                      
   &                                                                                
   SAMIS                                                                            
    WAL                                                                             
    TA                                                                              
    SYENROTTA                                                                       
              mean he wasn’t permitted to put quotes around anything except court opinions and statutes. (Id. at p.
              4-5)                                                                  
                  22.  In furtherance Mr. Dong’s arguments, he also explained that he had made
              unsuccessful attempts to meet with Professor Shorofsky about the paper’s subject prior to the
              paper’s submission; along with the misinterpretation of guidance given to him by Professor Ho, Mr.
              Dong had also understood advisement from research librarian USF Professor Michael Muehe to say
              that there was no need to Bluebook the research paper. (Id. at p. 5)  
                  23.  The Committee’s own report said that they ran the paper through the plagiarism
              software twice, with varying results and without citing to an actual threshold for the standard of
              evidence that “raises concerns.” (Id. at p. 1 – footnote No. 1)       
                  24.  Disregarding Mr. Dong’s defenses, the Honor Committee held that “the evidence
              demonstrates that Mr. Dong was repeatedly and specifically apprised of what plagiarism means and
              how to properly attribute unoriginal ideas” by virtue of Professor Shorofsky’s syllabus, Mr. Dong’s
              attendance of the LRWA course, and his ability to Bluebook.2 (Id. at p. 4)
                  25.  Ultimately, in the USF Honor Committee’s report they used Mr. Dong’s intelligence
              against him, stating that he was “bright” and should have understood the context of Professor Ho’s
              grading note due to his prior education.3 (Id. at p. 4-5)             
                  26.  When Mr. Dong’s counsel argued that there was no proof that he intended to violate
              the Honor Code, the Committee’s Report notes that they don’t need proof of his intent and "can
              establish culpability whenever a student violates its terms regardless of their mental state,”
              according to Section 4.1(b)(3) (Id. at p. 5) . The Committee further stated that Mr. Dong failed to
              meet his burden of proof of a preponderance of evidence that he inadvertently violated the Honor
              Code (Id. at p. 3-4).                                                 
              2 The LRWA course and Blue booking were both taught by Professor Ho who was the author of the note that Mr. Dong
              cites as the source of his confusion.                                 
              3 Mr. Dong’s background was in Economics, not in the language arts, history, or philosophy fields that many law
              students hail from.

               FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE, WRIT OF ORDINARY
                   MANDATE, REQUEST FOR STAY / PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND REQUEST FOR
                                       DECLARATORY RELIEF                           
   .DTL                                                                             
   ,SETAICOSSA                                                                      
   &                                                                                
   SAMIS                                                                            
    WAL                                                                             
    TA                                                                              
    SYENROTTA                                                                       
                  27.  The Committee Report found the charge “merited” and suspended Mr. Dong for the
              Fall semester of 2024, placing him on disciplinary probation, and a formal reprimand in his
              permanent record, but not his transcript. Further the decision requires Mr. Dong to complete the
              Indiana University Plagiarism Tutorials and Certification Test, and to redraft the subject paper
              under the terms of specifically assigned close reading and meeting with the Associate Dean or
              designee on the subjects of Ethical Use of Borrowed Materials, plagiarism, and proper attribution in
              legal writing. Additionally, he will be given no credit for the entire Art Law course.
                  28.  The Committee Report was disseminated to Mr. Dong, Dean, and Associate Dean,
              and both investigators.                                               
                  29.  On July 3, 2024, Mr. Dong formally appealed the Honor Committee Decision,
              stating procedural and substantive problems in the way the case was handled. (Mr. Dong’s Appeal
              of Honor Committee Decision)                                          
                  30.  Mr. Dong raised the issue of the erroneous application of the Turnitin report, the
              violation of the Committee permitting witnesses at the hearing, altered evidence at hearing, refusal
              to consider Mr. Dong’s defenses, and the Committee’s failure to adhere to confidentiality
              requirements about the hearing, (Mr. Dong’s Appeal of Honor Committee Decision, dated July 3,
              2024). Mr. Dong also raised USF’s violation of the honor code for disclosing the Committee Report
              to the Student and Faculty investigators. (Id.).                      
                  31.  On August 2, 2024, Respondent received a letter from Dean Johanna Kalb (“Dean
              Kalb”) denying Mr. Dong’s appeal and finding no errors or enough evidence to require reversible
              error. Dean Kalb addressed the inappropriate disclosure of the Committee Report to the
              investigators as “outside the scope of this appeal.” (Dean Kalb’s Response to Mr. Dong’s Appeal)
                  32.  Also on August 2, 2024, Dean Flynn emailed Mr. Dong stating “I have concluded
              that a possible violation occurred and am giving you the opportunity to meet with me to respond to
              the allegations. It was reported that you were seen looking at your phone in the bathroom while
              taking a final exam on May 4, 2024.” (Statement of Mr. Dong to USF Investigators, dated October
              16, 2024)

               FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE, WRIT OF ORDINARY
                   MANDATE, REQUEST FOR STAY / PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND REQUEST FOR
                                       DECLARATORY RELIEF                           
   .DTL                                                                             
   ,SETAICOSSA                                                                      
   &                                                                                
   SAMIS                                                                            
    WAL                                                                             
    TA                                                                              
    SYENROTTA                                                                       
                  33.  Mr. Dong fully denies the allegation that he looked at his cell phone in the bathroom
              during any exam.                                                      
                  34.  Mr. Dong was not aware that someone claimed to have looked at him in the
              bathroom and was only made aware when Dean Flynn informed him three months later.
                  35.  On August 30, 2024, Mr. Dong filed a Petition for Writ of Administrative Mandate,
              Petition for Writ of Ordinary Mandate, and Request for Declaratory Relief, with the San Francisco
              County Superior Court.                                                
                  36.  Mr. Dong filed a Title IX complaint against Dean Flynn claiming she had abused her
              discretion by failing to inform Mr. Dong of the bathroom voyeurism incident, harassment, and
              retaliation.                                                          
                  37.  The Title IX investigation is still an open matter. Dean Flynn, who Mr. Dong filed
              the Title IX matter against, is leading the investigation against him for the second alleged Honor
              Code violation.                                                       
                  38.  On September 12, 2024, Mr. Dong received an email from Professor Lindsay Harris
              that she and student Warren Vigneswaran were appointed by Dean Flynn to investigate the second
              alleged honor code violation against Mr. Dong.                        
                  39.  On September 26, 2024, Dean Flynn emailed Mr. Dong’s counsel, Frances E.
              Heredia, stating that Professor Lindsay Harris will be stepping aside due to a family emergency and
              Professor Alice Kaswan will be resuming the investigation.            
                  40.  Mr. Dong provided a statement to the investigators in lieu of an interview on October
              16, 2024, in which Mr. Dong denies the allegation, raises his concern that USF violated his right to
              privacy in the restroom, a place where he expects privacy, and states his concern that Dean Flynn
              has not recused herself in the investigation due to the conflict of interest.
                  41.  On October 21, 2024, Mr. Dong received an email from his employer, stating that
              they were informed he was suspended from USF and requested to speak with him to determine
              whether he could continue his employment.

               FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE, WRIT OF ORDINARY
                   MANDATE, REQUEST FOR STAY / PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND REQUEST FOR
                                       DECLARATORY RELIEF                           
   .DTL                                                                             
   ,SETAICOSSA                                                                      
   &                                                                                
   SAMIS                                                                            
    WAL                                                                             
    TA                                                                              
    SYENROTTA                                                                       
                  42.  On October 23, 2024, Mr. Dong spoke with his employer who told him that they
              were informed from a “USF Connection” that he was suspended, and they verified the information
              regarding Mr. Dong’s enrollment with the USF Registrar. Mr. Dong was terminated as a result of
              USF releasing Mr. Dong’s suspension to his employer.                  
                          PETITION FOR WRIT OF TRADITIONAL MANDATE                  
                                      (Code Civ. Proc., § 1085)                     
                  43.  Petitioner references paragraphs 1 through 42 above and incorporates them herein by
              reference as though fully set forth.                                  
                  44.  Petitioner has a beneficial interest in the outcome of the proceedings. (Code of Civil
              Procedure, § 1086.) An adverse result could permanently affect his professional reputation,
              permanently lose his current employment, void out accepted offer of employment as an associate
              attorney, extend his law school graduation date by one-year, potential licensure with the California
              State Bar, and impact his ability to get future work.                 
                  45.  Petitioner is seeking to compel Respondent to act pursuant to its own policies, rules,
              and procedures, in addition to statutory and other law. (Code of Civil Procedure, § 1085(a).)
              Respondent USF is an inferior tribunal, board, or person and the performance of the act(s) that the
              law specially enjoins, and has a duty resulting from an office, trust, or stations.
                  46.  Respondent has a clear, present, ministerial, and legal duty to only suspend a student
              where there is clear legal authority to do so and to protect the confidentiality its student body,
              including any disciplinary action against a student, from third parties without student permission.
                  47.  Petitioner contends that a suspension is not valid because the consequence is overly
              harsh. Mr. Dong is seeking the administrative remedy rather than accepting USF’s informal resolution,
              which required Mr. Dong to forgo any future claims against USF and admit to violating the school’s
              honor code, which he adamantly denies. USF is obligated to perform in a legally prescribed manner
              when these given facts exist and to perform these acts without exercise of discretion or opinion.
              This petition seeks to correct USF’s abuse of discretion or acts in excess of its legal powers,

              including correcting actions that are arbitrary, capriciously, or entirely without evidentiary support,
              and those that did not conform to law.                                
                  48.  An action in ordinary mandamus is proper where the claim is that an agency has
              failed to act as required by law.                                     
                  49.  Ordinary mandamus applies because the USF’s actions were arbitrary, capricious, or
              entirely lacking in evidentiary support, contrary to established public policy, unlawful, procedurally
              unfair, or the agency failed to follow the procedure and give the notices the law requires. (Code of
              Civil Procedure, §1085; City of Scotts Valley v. County of Santa Cruz (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 97;
              Jefferson v. Compton Unified School Dist. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 32.)  
                  50.  Respondent abused its discretion by suspending Petitioner from courses without
              evidentiary support to do so and in a manner that is against public policy.
                  51.  Respondent also abused its discretion by providing investigators with two options for
              concluding the investigation, in which both require a finding of guilt of Respondent, and not
              allowing a finding of no violation against Respondent as an option.   
                  52.  Respondent’s abuse of discretion has caused and threatens to cause Petitioner harm
              in that it failed to provide due process, failed to provide proper notice of the allegations against
              Petitioner, declined to weigh Respondents defenses to the allegations as permitted in the USF Honor
              Code, communicated confidential information regarding the Honor Code to unnecessary parties
              which caused Respondent harm, and authorized unnecessary witnesses to be present during
              testimony                                                             
                  53.  Respondent did not address their own honor code violation of disclosing the
              Committee Report to parties unauthorized to receive it and has continued to disclose the Committee
              Report’s findings to unnecessary parties, which continues to directly damage Respondent.
                  54.  Furthermore, USF claims that the Petitioner failed to meet the burden of proof
              established (preponderance of evidence) that he did not intend violate the Honor Code by leaving
              quotation marks out of his paper—this is untrue, as established above.
                  55.  Finally, USF has failed to establish that there was an intentional failure to attribute

              Procedure, § 1094.5.)                                                 
                  59.  USF proceeded without or in excess of its jurisdiction, and prejudicially abused its

              by law. (Code of Civil Procedure, § 1094.5(b).)

                  not omit citation of the article he used in his paper, his format for attribution was flawed.
                       b.   USF’s decision does not consider the fact that Petitioner did not dispute his
                  mistake, apologized, and expressed defensible reasons for misinterpreting guidelines and
                  rules given to him both verbally and in writing by USF staff.

                               REQUEST  FOR DECLARATORY  RELIEF                     
                                      (Code Civ. Proc., § 1060)                     
                  63.  Petitioner refer to paragraphs 1 through 62 above and incorporates the same by this
              reference as though fully set forth herein at length.                 
                  64.  Petitioner has an actual present controversy with USF regarding USF’s unilateral,
              unlawful and inappropriate enforcement of the Honor Code as described above which prevents
              Petitioner from lawfully conducting his business as a student at the USF School of Law.
                  65.  Petitioner brings this original action for a declaration of the parties’ rights and duties,
              given that USF acted outside the scope of its authority, and seeks a declaratory judgment or order
              addressing the following controversies and legal questions:           
                       a. Whether USF has failed to comply with the statutory and regulatory definitions of
                         its statutes and regulations regarding the eligibility of Petitioners to participate in
                         university activities;                                     
                       b. Whether USF has abused its discretion and acted in excess of its legal authority by
                         suspending Mr. Dong from USF School of Law for the Fall 2024 semester (among
                         the other punitive measures imposed);                      
                       c.                                             PRAYER                                  
              WHEREFORE, Petitioner Jialiang Dong prays for judgment as follows:    
                  1. That the court issue a writ of ordinary mandate under Code of Civil Procedure section
                     1085, directed to Respondent, compelling it to set aside its suspension of Mr. Dong and
                     his education and requiring him to perform the above-stated actions;
                  2. That the court issue a writ of administrative mandate under Code of Civil Procedure
                     section 1094.5, directed to Respondent, compelling it to set aside the suspension and all
                     entries in Mr. Dong’s permanent records and allowing Mr. Dong to participate in USF
                     sanctioned activities;                                         
                  3. That the court stay the suspension pending a hearing on the merits of the writ.
                  4. That the court issue a declaratory judgment deciding those issues outlined above in the
                     Request for Declaratory Relief;                                
                  5. For the Court to issue a temporary restraining order and preliminary injunction against
                     USF enjoining them from enforcing the suspension and disciplinary action against Mr.
                     Dong;                                                          
                  6. For costs of suit incurred herein, including reasonable fees for preparation of the
                     administrative record;
